items.q: Why does renewable energy require energy storage solutions?
items.correct: Solar and wind generation are intermittent - storage smooths out supply and demand.
items.misconception: Renewable energy can never meet demand.
items.random_wrong: Because they generate too much energy.
items.why: Students dismiss renewables as inadequate. The challenge is intermittency, not insufficiency - the Sun sets, wind drops. Storage (batteries, pumped hydro) addresses timing, not total capacity.

items.q: Why are top predators most affected by persistent pollutants like mercury?
items.correct: Biomagnification concentrates pollutants at each trophic level up the food chain.
items.misconception: Predators are exposed to more pollution directly.
items.why: Students focus on direct exposure. The mechanism is concentration through eating contaminated prey - small accumulations in lower levels multiply upward, so apex predators have the highest body burdens.

items.correct: Dissolved CO2 forms carbonic acid, lowering ocean pH.
items.misconception: Acid rain falling on the ocean.
items.why: Students think acidification is direct pollution. The main driver is atmospheric CO2 dissolving in seawater - an inevitable result of higher atmospheric concentrations, threatening shell-forming organisms.

items.q: The ecological footprint concept was introduced by Wackernagel and Rees in:
items.why: It's 1996. “1972” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Date annual biocapacity is exhausted. “Maximum population” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year ecological footprint concept was published?
items.why: The real figure is 1996 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
